steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must complete a logical inference from the evidence: after Deezer capped free listening at five hours a month, those free-tier users visited both licensed and unlicensed music-seller sites about 2% less. The inference must follow directly from that drop in music-purchase visits.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the restriction reduced the overall number of users': The passage tracks visits to music-seller sites by existing free users, not whether users left the service. No data on total user count. ✗
  • Choice B — 'restrictions on streaming may suppress interest in owning music': A 2% drop in visits to music-purchase sites directly shows that limiting free streaming reduced users' drive to seek music ownership elsewhere. ✓
  • Choice C — 'users were encouraged to move to the paid tier': The passage says nothing about users upgrading; it only tracks free users' visits to external seller sites. ✗
  • Choice D — 'consumers are unlikely to buy physical copies of readily streamed music': The study tracks digital music-seller visits and unlicensed download sites, not physical media. Physical copies are not addressed. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
